title: rubus tomentosus f. ribesifolius
intro: Rubus tomentosus f. ribesifolius, also known as Himalayan raspberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region. It is a variant of the Rubus tomentosus species and is characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are said to have various health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Rubus tomentosus f. ribesifolius is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and derive its health benefits.
